name: Arminius (Hermann)
role: Germanic Chieftain, Commander at Teutoburg Forest (9 CE)
quote: We fight not as slaves but as free men of Germania — the legions shall not pass the Rhine.

name: Charlemagne (Karl der Große)
role: King of the Franks, first Holy Roman Emperor (742–814)
quote: To have another language is to possess a second soul — education is the great unifier of my realm.

name: Martin Luther
role: Theologian, Father of the Protestant Reformation (1483–1546)
quote: Here I stand, I can do no other. God help me. Amen. — Diet of Worms, 1521

name: Frederick the Great (Friedrich II)
role: King of Prussia 1740–1786, military reformer and Enlightenment monarch
quote: The first duty of a king is to maintain himself in his post — everything else flows from power well-used.

name: Otto von Bismarck
role: Minister-President of Prussia, first Chancellor of united Germany (1815–1898)
quote: Politics is the art of the possible, the attainable — the art of the next best. — 1867

name: Kaiser Wilhelm II
role: German Emperor 1888–1918, presided over WWI and abdication
quote: I no longer recognize parties; I recognize only Germans. — August 4, 1914

name: Rosa Luxemburg
role: Marxist theorist, co-founder of KPD, murdered 1919
quote: Freedom is always the freedom of those who think differently. — 1918

name: Konrad Adenauer
role: First Chancellor of the Federal Republic of Germany 1949–1963 (CDU)
quote: We all live under the same sky, but we don't all have the same horizon. — On European integration

name: Walter Ulbricht
role: First Secretary of Socialist Unity Party (SED), GDR leader 1950–1971
quote: Nobody intends to build a wall. — June 15, 1961, weeks before the Wall was built

name: Willy Brandt
role: SPD Chancellor 1969–1974, architect of Ostpolitik, Nobel Peace Prize 1971
quote: Wir wollen mehr Demokratie wagen — We want to risk more democracy. — Inaugural address, 1969

name: Helmut Schmidt
role: SPD Chancellor 1974–1982, statesman and NATO double-track decision architect
quote: Those who have visions should see a doctor. — Attributed, on political pragmatism

name: Helmut Kohl
role: CDU Chancellor 1982–1998, architect of German reunification and Euro
quote: German unity and European unity are two sides of the same coin. — 1990

name: Erich Honecker
role: General Secretary of SED and GDR State Council Chairman 1971–1989
quote: The Wall will be standing in fifty and even in one hundred years, if the reasons for it are not removed. — January 1989

name: Gerhard Schröder
role: SPD Chancellor 1998–2005, Agenda 2010 and Hartz reforms, later Nord Stream advocate
quote: Germany's national interest is to integrate Russia into European structures. — 2001

name: Angela Merkel
role: CDU Chancellor 2005–2021, Europe's longest-serving leader; 'Wir schaffen das'
quote: Wir schaffen das — We can do this. — August 31, 2015, on refugee policy

name: Olaf Scholz
role: SPD Chancellor 2021–2025, traffic-light coalition; declared Zeitenwende after Ukraine invasion
quote: This is a Zeitenwende — an epochal tectonic shift. — Bundestag speech, February 27, 2022

name: Friedrich Merz
role: CDU Chancellor from February 2025, CDU party leader since 2022
quote: We will restore Germany's competitiveness and reduce irregular migration — with or without coalition comfort. — Feb 2025

name: Alice Weidel
role: AfD co-leader and Chancellor candidate, 2025 federal election (20.8% vote share)
quote: Germany is heading in the wrong direction — only the AfD represents the real will of German citizens. — 2025 campaign

name: Ursula von der Leyen
role: European Commission President (CDU), former German Defence Minister 2013–2019
quote: Europe's geopolitical era has arrived — we must speak the language of power. — 2019

name: Robert Habeck
role: Green Party co-leader, Federal Economics and Climate Minister 2021–2025
quote: We cannot rebuild an industrial democracy without transforming our energy system fundamentally. — 2023
